name: Bec du Gave Loop
name_original: Confluence de l'Adour et Les Gaves Réunis – boucle dans Sainte-Marie-de-Gosse
description: Hike a moderate 7.4-mile loop in Sainte-Marie-de-Gosse, exploring the scenic confluence of the Adour and Gaves Réunis rivers.
region_intro: Hiking around Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse offers a network of trails exploring the region's varied terrain. The landscape is characterized by its proximity to rivers, featuring flat to gently rolling paths often alongside waterways. Hikers can expect a mix of rural scenery, agricultural lands, and occasional small hills. The area provides accessible routes suitable for different fitness levels, connecting villages and natural features. Best hiking trails around Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse The most popular hiking route is Bec du Gave Loop, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area around the confluence of rivers. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gravel Path Along the River – Fitness Trail loop from Urt, a moderate 6.8 miles (10.9 km) path. This trail follows a gravel path alongside a river, offering a flat and accessible experience. Local hikers also love the La Galupe Restaurant – Ardanavy pedestrian bridge loop from Urt, a 3.6 miles (5.7 km) trail leading through riverside and village landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 28 minutes. Hiking around Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse is defined by riverside paths, rural landscapes, and gentle elevation changes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and some more challenging routes. The routes in Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse?
A: There are over 40 hiking routes in the Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse area, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and some more challenging options.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse?
A: Yes, Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse offers 14 easy routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed stroll. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41499966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Galupe Restaurant – Ardanavy pedestrian bridge loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.6 miles (5.7 km) and takes about 1 hour 28 minutes through riverside and village landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse?
A: The landscape around Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse is characterized by its proximity to rivers, offering mostly flat to gently rolling paths, often alongside waterways. You'll experience a mix of rural scenery, agricultural lands, and occasional small hills.

Q: Are there any circular walks available?
A: Many routes in the region are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993769548/bec-du-gave-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bec du Gave Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) moderate trail exploring the confluence of rivers. Another great circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39253493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel Path Along the River – Fitness Trail loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking near Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse?
A: The area offers several interesting points. You can explore the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4838365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saubusse&lt;/a&gt;, see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1053645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saubusse Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5248143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bastide of Hastingues&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also pass through &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6631089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urt&lt;/a&gt;, a notable local village.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options?
A: Many of the easier and moderate routes are suitable for families. The gentle terrain along rivers and through rural landscapes makes for pleasant walks. Consider shorter loop trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41499966" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Galupe Restaurant – Ardanavy pedestrian bridge loop from Urt&lt;/a&gt; for a manageable family outing.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse?
A: The trails in Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the peaceful riverside paths, the varied rural scenery, and the accessibility of routes for different fitness levels.

Q: Is there public transport access to hiking trailheads in the region?
A: While specific public transport links directly to every trailhead can vary, some routes, particularly those starting from larger villages like Urt, may be more accessible via local bus services. It's advisable to check local transport schedules for specific starting points.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse?
A: Parking is generally available in or near the villages where many trails begin, such as Urt or Hastingues. For specific trailheads, look for designated parking areas or roadside parking options, especially for popular routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36034101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porte de Hastingues – Bastide of Hastingues loop from Hastingues&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any longer distance trails for experienced hikers?
A: While the region is known for its accessible routes, there are moderate trails that offer a longer experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993769548/bec-du-gave-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bec du Gave Loop&lt;/a&gt; at 7.4 miles (11.9 km) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24683714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Different landscapes loop from Saint-Martin-de-Hinx&lt;/a&gt; at 7 miles (11.2 km) provide good options for those seeking a longer day out.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Sainte-Marie-De-Gosse?
A: The region is generally pleasant for hiking during spring, summer, and autumn. Spring brings blooming landscapes, while autumn offers beautiful colors. Summers are warm, making riverside paths particularly enjoyable. Winter hikes are also possible, though some paths might be muddier.
tips: A serene place to overlook and appreciate the two rivers. Depending on the time of year, it is possible to see the difference in color of the water.

faqs.question: Where does the Confluence of the Adour and Les Gaves Réunis loop trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a loop in Sainte-Marie-de-Gosse. While the exact starting point isn't specified, it's generally accessible within the commune. Parking information is not detailed in the available data, but typically, trails in such areas have designated parking near the trailhead or in the village center.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is classified as moderate and is generally suitable for all levels of hikers, though a good physical condition is recommended. Its varied scenery and accessible paths make it a pleasant option for families.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse scenery year-round. Winter is particularly notable for observing thousands of migratory birds like egrets, herons, and storks in the 'Barthes' (flooded meadows). Spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, while summer provides lush views of cornfields and kiwi orchards.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Confluence of the Adour and Les Gaves Réunis loop?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify rules regarding dogs on this particular trail. It's always best to check local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding dog-friendliness and leash requirements before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to hike this loop trail in Sainte-Marie-de-Gosse. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied terrain, including paths through rolling valleys, prairies, cornfields, and kiwi orchards. The paths are generally easily accessible. While some sections may be 'too road-like' for some, these roads are not typically busy.

faqs.question: What natural features and wildlife can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers proximity to the Adour River and the Gaves Réunis. You'll encounter the 'Barthes,' vast flooded meadows rich in wildlife, including geese, horses, cows, and various bird species like egrets, herons, and storks, especially in winter. The Bec de Gave, where the Adour and Gaves meet, is a significant natural highlight.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks on the trail?
faqs.answer: A key viewpoint is the Bec de Gave, which provides a surprising perspective of the confluence of the Gaves Réunis and the Adour River. You'll also pass through kiwi fields and experience the scenic river views.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of a larger network of trails in the Landes area. Specifically, it covers sections of the EV3 France 47, Dax - Urt (57% of the route), and the Route des Berges (8% of the route).

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities near the trailhead in Sainte-Marie-de-Gosse?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not detail specific cafes, pubs, or accommodation directly at the trailhead. However, as the trail is located in Sainte-Marie-de-Gosse, you would likely find amenities within the village itself or nearby communes.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, comfortable hiking shoes are recommended. Depending on the season, consider layers for changing weather, water, and snacks. Binoculars could enhance wildlife viewing, especially in winter.

waypoints.name: Bec de Gave: where the Adour and the Gaves meet
